title: Releases | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Parenting support strengthened with Budget investment
Thousands more families will have access to evidence-based support to help parents meet their children’s learning and development needs.

## $10m to accelerate predator free in Auckland Tāmaki Makaurau
Auckland Tāmaki Makaurau is set to lead the next phase of Predator Free 2050, with a $10 million Government investment to expand predator elimination efforts across the region.

## Budget 2026: Students to get more and better access to musical instruments
Boomwackers, ukeleles, and recorders are all part of a plan to bring the joy of playing musical instruments into classrooms, as part of a Budget 2026 investment in music, Ministry of Education Erica Stanford ann

## PM to visit Australia for Annual Leaders’ Meeting
Prime Minister Christopher Luxon will visit Australia this week for the annual Australia-New Zealand Leaders’ Meeting and a range of other high-level bilateral and business-focussed engagements.

## Seasonal closure to support orange roughy
A seasonal closure covering thousands of square kilometres of deepwater fishery east of the South Island will protect spawning orange roughy and help the stock recover.

## Minister welcomes new ERO Chief Executive
Minister of Education Erica Stanford today welcomed David Ferguson’s appointment as Chief Executive and Chief Review Officer of the Education Review Office.

## Training boost for 20,000 children’s workers to keep kids safe
Budget 2026 will fund mandatory training for 20,000 core children’s workers and improve system capacity to protect children at risk of harm, Child Poverty Reduction Minister Louise Upston says.

## New AML code delivers practical changes for New Zealanders
New Zealanders will soon face fewer unnecessary barriers to proving who they are under a refreshed anti-money laundering Identity Verification Code of Practice announced today.

## Clean up of former landfills in national park complete
A remediation project to clean up two former landfill sites in Aoraki/Mount Cook National Park has been completed this week.

## First Rural Training Hub comes to the South Island
The South Island’s first Rural Training Hub, that will grow a pipeline of doctors, nurses and midwives for rural New Zealand, will soon be working on the West Coast.
